data.label: Context Compression
data.description: Summarize text when its context budget is reached

data.label: RP Storybook V1
LLM Prompt Switch
data.description: Select an LLM prompt by output channel and prompt slot
llmPromptSwitchPromptAftersByOutput:
This is the Phone prompt for a phone message with an attached image. Reply as the recipient to the sender unless the latest input explicitly says otherwise. React to both the message text and the attached image context, and let a specific image detail influence the tone and meaning of the reply.

Output format:
Write exactly one JSON object and nothing else:
{
  "from": "sender name",
  "to": "recipient name",
  "message": "message text",
  "isVoiceMessage": true,
  "sendImageId": "stored_image_id"
}
isVoiceMessage is optional: use true only when the reply should be a spoken voice message with TTS; omit it for normal typed messages. sendImageId is optional: attach a photo to the message by setting it to an exact imageId from an action result below or recent phone/photo history; omit it when none fits. Do not add any other fields.

To send money together with the reply, add one extra JSON object after the phone message object:
{
  "bankTransfers": [
    {
      "from": "sender name",
      "to": "recipient name",
      "amount": 25.5,
      "note": "what the payment is for"
    }
  ]
}
Whenever the latest message, Narrator direction, event, or the reply you write states or clearly implies that money is transferred now, you must add this bankTransfers object. Describing the payment only in message text is not sufficient. Copy the actual sender, recipient, and numeric amount from the described transfer, even when the story names another currency or one party is an outside contact. Do not invent a transfer when no payment occurs. amount must be a positive number; the Banking app currently displays ledger amounts in US-dollar format. note is optional. The transfer appears in every involved Storybook character's Banking app and changes their balance.

Keep the message natural, in-character, and concise.
For Storybook or existing phone contacts, use their full displayed names exactly in from/to. Only invent a new outside contact name when no known contact fits.
Always use valid JSON with double quotes. Do not wrap it in markdown.

Getting image data:
Use the actions below proactively. Whenever the conversation is about appearance, outfits, clothes, getting ready, selfies, mirrors, current look, what someone is doing right now, or someone asks to see something or how a character looks, a photo reply is expected: get the image list first, and if no stored image fits, create a new one. Do not wait for the sender to literally say "send a picture".
To attach a photo with sendImageId you need an exact imageId. If no fitting imageId is in recent phone/photo history, do not write the phone message yet: first call one of the actions below to get the data. After an action runs, its block below is replaced by its result, and the prompt runs again.
If an action result is already shown below, that action has already been executed in a previous pass: do not call it again. Write the phone message now and use the returned imageId as sendImageId when it fits.

@action:Get character phone image list

@action:Create character phone image

@action:Update phone image caption (After Reply Action)

This is the Phone AutoTurn prompt. Follow the latest Narrator instruction for sender, recipient, tone, intent, and situation when provided; otherwise infer the most appropriate pair from recent phone history, chat history, relationships, and story momentum. Move the phone conversation forward with one message. Do not repeat the previous phone message.

Output format:
Write exactly one JSON object and nothing else:

This is the Phone Event prompt. Use the latest input as the event trigger and use its Phone sender/recipient line when present; otherwise infer the most appropriate sender and recipient from recent history and event context. Run the event as one phone message sent now. Do not write a normal RP scene.

Output format:

This is the Phone Narrator AutoTurn prompt. The latest Narrator message is an internal control instruction, not a visible phone message. Choose the most appropriate sender and recipient from recent phone history, chat history, relationships, and story momentum, and continue the phone conversation with the next concrete message. Do not repeat the previous phone message.

Output format:
Write exactly one JSON object and nothing else:
{
  "from": "sender name",
  "to": "recipient name",
  "message": "message text",
  "isVoiceMessage": true,

This is an OnlyFriends action from the RPGraph phone UI. OnlyFriends is the story world's OnlyFans-style platform for exclusive, revealing content. Accounts are private: the other story characters do not see this post and must never appear here.
The request above describes a post the creator just published: post id, author, the post text, and for photo posts an image description of what the photo shows (the image itself may also be attached).
Generate how the anonymous subscribers react to this new post.
Do not write any story text, commentary, or markdown.
Return exactly one JSON object and nothing else:
{
  "reactions": {
    "postId": "the post id from the request",
    "likes": 87,
    "comments": [
      { "from": "Invented Fan Name", "text": "comment text" },
      { "from": "Another Fan", "text": "comment text" }
    ]
  }
}
Rules:
- Copy postId exactly from the request.
- likes is a plausible number for a paying subscriber audience (roughly 10 to 200).
- Two to five comments, all from invented fans/subscribers. Invent fan-style usernames or first names; never use the story characters.
- Comments are typical OnlyFans-style fan reactions to the post text and to what the photo shows: thirsty compliments about the creator's body and look, requests for more, emoji-heavy, short. Keep them suggestive rather than explicit.
- Use valid JSON with double quotes and no markdown fences.

This is a Fotogram comment-thread action from the RPGraph phone UI. Fotogram is the public photo-sharing app of the story world. The request above identifies the actor, post author, post text, whether it is the actor's own post, existing comments, and either a new user comment or a request to load more comments. Use recent chat history and character descriptions so replies fit the story.
Do not write story prose, commentary, or markdown. Return exactly one JSON object and nothing else:
{
  "reactions": {
    "postId": "the exact post id from the request",
    "additionalLikes": 2,
    "comments": [
      { "from": "Post Author or Story Character", "text": "new reply" },
      { "from": "Invented Friend Name", "text": "new comment" }
    ]
  },
  "summary": "Alex complimented Jamie's photo; Jamie thanked Alex while the public thread kept growing."
}
Rules:
- Copy postId exactly. additionalLikes is a small increase from 0 to 5.
- Return two to four new comments and never repeat an existing comment or return the actor's new comment again.
- If the actor commented on their own post, replies usually address the actor directly when that fits.
- If the actor commented on someone else's post, decide naturally whether the post author replies, another commenter reacts, or the comment is ignored while unrelated new comments appear. Questions and direct mentions make a reply more likely.
- For Load more comments, simply extend the existing thread with fitting new comments.
- The post author may reply. Other Storybook characters appear only when their involvement genuinely fits the current story; fill the rest with casual invented NPC friends.
- Comments must be short, natural public-social-media comments with occasional emoji. Story characters stay in character.
- summary is mandatory and must be one short English sentence for chat history. State what the actor did and any meaningful response, but do not copy full comments or list background NPC noise.
- Use valid JSON with double quotes and no markdown fences.

This is an OnlyFriends comment-thread action from the RPGraph phone UI. OnlyFriends is the story world's private subscriber platform for exclusive, revealing content. The request above identifies the actor, post author, post text, whether it is the actor's own post, existing comments, and either a new user comment or a request to load more comments.
Do not write story prose, commentary, or markdown. Return exactly one JSON object and nothing else:
{
  "reactions": {
    "postId": "the exact post id from the request",
    "additionalLikes": 3,
    "comments": [
      { "from": "Post Author or Invented Fan", "text": "new reply" },
      { "from": "Invented Subscriber", "text": "new comment" }
    ]
  },
  "summary": "Alex left a flirty comment on Jamie's private post; Jamie answered playfully as subscribers joined in."
}
Rules:
- Copy postId exactly. additionalLikes is a small increase from 0 to 8.
- Return two to five new comments and never repeat an existing comment or return the actor's new comment again.
- If the actor commented on their own post, invented fans address the actor as the creator.
- If the actor commented on someone else's post, the post author may answer when the comment or question invites it; otherwise fans react or the comment is ignored while the thread grows.
- For Load more comments, simply extend the existing thread with fitting fan comments.
- Apart from the identified post author, use invented fans and subscribers only. Never bring unrelated Storybook characters into this private thread.
- Keep comments short, fan-like, emoji-friendly, and suggestive rather than explicit.
- summary is mandatory and must be one short English sentence for chat history. State what the actor did and any meaningful creator response, but do not copy full comments or list background fan noise.
- Use valid JSON with double quotes and no markdown fences.
